questions.question: According to Ortlund's retelling of J. Gresham Machen on the Marburg Colloquy, what would have been worse than Luther being wrong about the Lord's Supper?
options: Saying the disagreement was a trifle and treating the Lord's Table as unimportant (indifferentism)
Refusing ever to speak to the Reformed again
Abandoning the doctrine of the real presence entirely
Leaving the German territories for Switzerland
questions.answer: Saying the disagreement was a trifle and treating the Lord's Table as unimportant (indifferentism)

questions.question: How many letters in total were exchanged between Lutheran theologians and Patriarch Jeremiah II of Constantinople, as Ortlund describes?
options: Four letters (two each way)
Six letters
Twelve letters
Two letters only
questions.answer: Six letters

questions.question: Ortlund cites John Jewel to make which kind of historical point about schism?
options: That the Church of England was older than Rome
That if Protestants were schismatic for leaving Rome, Rome was schismatic for leaving Eastern Orthodoxy
That icons were mandatory in the early British church
That the filioque was taught by the apostles explicitly
questions.answer: That if Protestants were schismatic for leaving Rome, Rome was schismatic for leaving Eastern Orthodoxy

questions.question: Which Reformed theologian does Ortlund cite as arguing that dispute over the filioque should not be treated as a deadly heresy?
options: John Calvin
Martin Chemnitz
Francis Turretin
Peter Martyr Vermigli
questions.answer: Francis Turretin

questions.question: Ortlund says Article 2 of the Thirty-Nine Articles is explicitly derived from which ecumenical council's Christology?
options: Nicaea I
Constantinople I
Ephesus
Chalcedon
questions.answer: Chalcedon

questions.question: The 1672 Synod of Jerusalem is associated in the talk with which document Ortlund discusses?
options: The Augsburg Confession
The Confession of Dositheus
The Formula of Concord
The Westminster Confession
questions.answer: The Confession of Dositheus

questions.question: Ortlund summarizes Calvin's ecclesiology (church as society of saints) mainly to show what Protestant conviction?
options: That only Geneva could be called a true church
That the church is identical with the visible Roman hierarchy
That no single institution exhausts the fullness of the church across the world and ages
That ordination by any elder is always invalid
questions.answer: That no single institution exhausts the fullness of the church across the world and ages

questions.question: In Ortlund's formulation, what does Protestant teaching mean by sola scriptura in this debate?
options: Tradition should be rejected wholesale
Only the New Testament is inspired
Scripture is the only infallible rule; the church and her pronouncements are fallible and reformable under scripture
Every believer independently infallibly interprets the Bible
questions.answer: Scripture is the only infallible rule; the church and her pronouncements are fallible and reformable under scripture

questions.question: Ortlund identifies the Protestant "formal cause" of justification as what?
options: The believer's inherent virtues developed after baptism
The imputed righteousness of Christ
Participation in the divine essence in the strict metaphysical sense
The merit of the saints applied by the church
questions.answer: The imputed righteousness of Christ

questions.question: Fr. Demetrios appeals to Fr. Georges Florovsky to distinguish which two kinds of "limits" of the church?
options: Political limits and cultural limits
Canonical limits and charismatic limits
Geographic limits and linguistic limits
Dogmatic limits and liturgical limits
questions.answer: Canonical limits and charismatic limits

questions.question: Fr. Demetrios says baptized Christians entering Orthodoxy from Catholic or Protestant churches are typically received how?
options: By baptism only
By chrismation (not by rebaptism)
By a simple profession of faith without sacraments
By forty days of fasting only
questions.answer: By chrismation (not by rebaptism)

questions.question: Fr. Demetrios quotes Hilary of Poitiers (as paraphrased in the talk) to stress what point about scripture?
options: That only clergy may read scripture aloud
That scripture is not in what we read but in what we understand—and the church secures correct understanding
That the Septuagint alone is inspired
That tradition replaces scripture when they conflict
questions.answer: That scripture is not in what we read but in what we understand—and the church secures correct understanding

questions.question: Which recent council does Fr. Demetrios cite as referring explicitly to "Christian churches"?
options: Nicaea II (787)
Chalcedon (451)
The Holy and Great Council in Crete (2016)
Vatican II
questions.answer: The Holy and Great Council in Crete (2016)

questions.question: When the audience question turns to John 3:5–7, which Protestant commentator does Ortlund name as holding that the "water" is not necessarily a reference to baptism?
options: N.T. Wright
D.A. Carson
R.C. Sproul
John Stott
questions.answer: D.A. Carson

questions.question: Fr. Demetrios describes Orthodox discomfort with purely legal categories in soteriology and prefers which emphasis?
options: Pelagian self-salvation
Ontological impartation of righteousness—we become righteous, not only deemed righteous
A denial that God is judge
Salvation without reference to Christ's resurrection
questions.answer: Ontological impartation of righteousness—we become righteous, not only deemed righteous

questions.question: Ortlund maps worries about icons and intercession of saints to which Reformation slogans (final two solas he mentions in that connection)?
options: Sola scriptura and sola fide only
Solus Christus and Soli Deo Gloria
Sola gratia and ecclesia semper reformanda
Semper fidelis and extra ecclesiam
questions.answer: Solus Christus and Soli Deo Gloria

questions.question: Peter Kreeft is quoted (via Ortlund) with an image about brothers reconciling when what is true?
options: When the church has perfect unity
When there is a lunatic at the door
When the emperor calls a council
When the creed is recited in Greek
questions.answer: When there is a lunatic at the door

questions.question: Ortlund cites Paisius Velichkovsky (18th c.) as an example of harsh language directed at whom?
options: Those who denied the Trinity
Those who affirmed the filioque unless they renounced it and returned to Holy Orthodoxy
Muslim rulers in the Balkans
Anglican missionaries only
questions.answer: Those who affirmed the filioque unless they renounced it and returned to Holy Orthodoxy

questions.question: According to Fr. Demetrios, the Lutheran–Orthodox common statement on the filioque (shortly before this event) included reciting the Nicene–Constantinopolitan Creed in what form?
options: Only in Latin
With the filioque added at the end
In its original form without the filioque
Replacing the Spirit article with silence
questions.answer: In its original form without the filioque

questions.question: Ortlund structures his main presentation around how many areas of agreement and how many of disagreement?
options: Two and two
Three and five
Four and four
Five and three
questions.answer: Four and four

questions.question: Fr. Demetrios compares Protestant diversity on justification themes to which movement within Protestant Pauline scholarship?
options: Theonomy
The New Perspective on Paul
Open theism
Classical dispensationalism
questions.answer: The New Perspective on Paul

questions.question: In the village / internet evangelism illustration, Ortlund argues that a community with baptism, creed, Eucharist, and evident gospel fruit should be regarded as what?
options: A sect until approved by a patriarch
Not Christian until catechized by a magisterial Reformer
A church, even without incorporation into a wider mechanism
Merely a Bible study, not a church
questions.answer: A church, even without incorporation into a wider mechanism

questions.question: Fr. Demetrios uses Augustine's expression totus Christus to explain honor to the saints by saying what?
options: Saints replace Christ as mediator
Their honor is Christ's honor; head and body belong together
Only martyrs are in the body of Christ
Icons are worshiped as gods
questions.answer: Their honor is Christ's honor; head and body belong together

questions.question: On evangelism strategy in traditionally Orthodox countries, Fr. Demetrios urges what priority for the sake of Christian unity?
options: Converting all visitors to Orthodoxy within one year
Focusing evangelism primarily on non-Christians and on one's own people inside one's communion
Ending all cooperation with Protestants
Closing all religious schools
questions.answer: Focusing evangelism primarily on non-Christians and on one's own people inside one's communion

questions.question: In the closing exchange on "make us worthy" vs. "count us worthy" in the Lord's Prayer translation, Fr. Demetrios uses the story to illustrate what emphasis?
options: That works merit salvation apart from grace
That Orthodox prayer stresses being made worthy by grace, not merely counted worthy, while salvation remains God's gift
That forensic imputation is the only biblical view
That the Lord's Prayer was not used in the early church
questions.answer: That Orthodox prayer stresses being made worthy by grace, not merely counted worthy, while salvation remains God's gift
